textByHash.dfd29bac39dd8d18f5426d2b36a124cfd8108dfb: Ha! Atarischeray! Blah blah mesi blah blah.
textByHash.5891e3b65cd72707773e9e376978f4d2e6181c31: Blah Brimstail blah blah human blah minahar!
textByHash.2edb10ab7cb5257389402bf0f60cc087f04b46cf: Hmmph. Laquinay blah human. Blah blah blah.
textByHash.92f567a424e668f2d8561a594f2578183f746109: Oaknock! Ha blah blah minapor blah blah blah.
textByHash.f08dbe253ff52c93bbf60d846c9f5f49763f3541: Blah blah blah blah Oaknock blah blah blah.
textByHash.becdfea6cc1adbd3dfb76e3463448bc1b41093e5: Blah blah blah blah Healthorg cinqo blah blah blah.
textByHash.c4ea2f791d14ebf5c2521be3cfedb469fea9ca32: ... ethereal link ... failed ... puny human mind ... insufficiently disciplined ... mental rigour required ... trained ... magical skills ...
textByHash.22432cf4929f8161073e30299fb8a9e3966a8de3: Hmph. Not bad for a human.
textByHash.8411c6cab93e99a59efb6eae165b9c27fa0e791a: Old magicks beyond your skill human. You can stop flapping your big human lips as well. Just think.
textByHash.80e77584d84e8d7bf76ae59bca6796fedfc91518: Stop wittering on . You're giving me a headache. This mind speech is hard enough without having to listen to your incoherent babbling. Think about nothing. Clear your mind.
textByHash.8ccc7bf685842130499a0fc9dc5a57c570b48e2a: I'm reading your mind foolish human. How do you think? Now CLEAR YOUR MIND...
textByHash.e1a0da44d2184fc2b977dd36b4ab8c69e0ad73e8: Just shut up and listen! I have a story to tell you that will answer most of your questions.
textByHash.da14075180a9b7d0b931fb7d09116eb254e9afbe: Let us travel back in time, to the time you humans call the Fourth Age. King Healthorg had become King. The elves mysteriously decided to travel west, leaving most of Kandarin open for conquest. The humans weren't here yet, only King Healthorg and the gnomes to the north, and a collection of Goblin tribes to the south.
textByHash.f67cf7aed6938ea2e13fe4d9b5fb6d8898f39a9e: Both armies moved to claim the lands, and were of course destined to meet in battle...
textByHash.79eab0147cff4263e721bd7ea9af88c50fb92a09: And so it came to pass the Goblins and Gnome armies met at the battle of Atarisundri.
textByHash.86f0fb1a2264cdac3ce063db7a28a84f842cb364: Both sides faced each other on the Battlefield. A bloody battle was in prospect, our gnomes were brave, but we were no match for the mass of brute goblin stupidity...
textByHash.de9246a7d55509dfe1c8bedcd52530bfc4092dc0: ... but Glouphrie and King Healthorg had devised a plan...
textByHash.4ecc5f2ea4d836ecb8d6f0364f4e8040dd985b0f: ... If you don't lead an invincible army - pretend you do...
textByHash.2bf026aa2488a66a88ea32f50671055c5aed6c3f: And so, Glouphrie created an army of illusory Giant tortoises, knowing that goblins would be scared of anything bigger than they were.
textByHash.ec76e86064d8777557012da1c0e24f49378907af: Handsome! Hmph! That was a long time ago...
textByHash.165c4562ca21eb66a1c2527fecc9fa75a0d03a75: Anyway, where was I? Oh yes...
textByHash.654f617c55d220cb0b62ea3a3041a1530622a64c: Sadly, the golden times were not destined to last for long...
textByHash.5d1a2597e1c27ed1f562a2fff969012e4e0c5458: There was once a very special Spirit Tree in the Stronghold. Silver as a birch, old and wise - I considered him a friend. I called him Argento, his true name was unpronouncable.
textByHash.48eb30736d29f829365b944578402e8272753aac: He was the first Spirit Tree I had befriended, and the one I knew the best. I would sometimes travel, visiting and finding other Spirit trees, and during one such trip, I was filled with a great sense of foreboding...
textByHash.9ac66672fe76eec5c6952d7c07471cf757ae3a1b: I felt a shadow pass over me, and a tingling in the air, even though the weather was fine and dry.
textByHash.90314a935bd2ebaa8c240bcb2a8c275d82b1b221: Then I felt a sudden pain, as if the Anima Mundi was crying out with the loss of a favoured child...
textByHash.315b28d8c0d5abd8efd605f6b25ff09fb2290b90: And I knew that one of the Old Ones had died, and I had lost a friend.
textByHash.fb217ead97313d0e0ed1dfacef0a47f952b08e36: I rushed straight back to the Grand Tree, only to find that no-one had noticed anything, and that Argento was still standing and seemed alive. But I knew something was wrong. He refused to talk to me and his aura was
textByHash.39eb8ac12be858cb5d015ceabcfab9e78c4888b1: no longer present. Someone had hidden his death. I talked to Oaknock and he was incensed. He immediately blamed Glouphrie, and although Oaknock could be rather irrational when it came to Glouphrie -
textByHash.fa33018d6fb4ecd37b7c9543d0e7951d7060f67f: they never saw eye to eye - I knew he was right this time. Glouphrie had used his magicks to hide the truth from all of us.
textByHash.de82b514cae3e66e4745fb14ffed7f50cb03c439: Oaknock had made a machine - an anti-illusion machine, with some help from the elves. He prepared it, and summoned the advisors to Argento..
textByHash.9698020a504fb3f80bd5e83b0318afadb79cafb5: And so Glouphrie left and travelled with some companions to a place they called Arposandra. We have heard nothing but rumours about them since...
textByHash.a32eb705ab7269bab8e0221c09281a730964c36d: Nobody knows for certain, somewhere rocky I suppose. The rumour is that after founding his new home, Glouphrie used his magicks to shroud the city in mist. Arposandra may not exist at all, however I suspect that
textByHash.2fa47cd78a2ca8d7b071caaf4fb502b648518457: Glouphrie is still around, one way or another.
textByHash.954f263b8e48b839e1adf9eb558dbc6b52ae5d7a: I never studied it, but I believe it used elven magic - So it must have used crystals of some sort. Crystal discs, yes that sounds familiar. Actually come to think of it I think I may have one lying around. Here take
textByHash.b31d1d4536b582f4c062681885026c55430eeed4: Very well. Oh and when you see that lazy toad Brimstail, tell him next time he needs information to come himself.
textByHash.0661ffcbf179e8caf165157d15330882695ed030: What do you want now? Go and talk to Brimstail, that's why you came to see me in the first place...
textByHash.5883a8a2ad83023e1bfa62b0efd13ffa458e0df5: What? What do you want? You shouldn't disturb me like that. I was deep in contemplation. Looking into the future is not something you can do on a whim, you know!
textByHash.851ae461714b1403b8479ef54ee23adb8b80ca76: Yes of course! You did call for me, didn't you? And what did I tell you about flapping your lips during a mind meld? Use your brain!
textByHash.3a6e441ae071ec1b7c9a5dffd4f9a4d08b8133f6: Now, this had best be important. I was in the middle of a dream, a vision of the future. There was something I was meant to see...
textByHash.8aa05f2ba8b56b080083adb121de27345ce31b42: Hmph! How did you manage to perform a mind meld from so far away anyway? Such skills are far beyond a human. Unless...
textByHash.c4f096b752e07e2dd89092e3caa75843ecd0b4a5: Of course, a spirit tree. I sense this one to be rather unwell. What happened?
textByHash.2012a10416e95913c28bc30ba9d3fac0dd24ebbd: Alright, let me try to sense the problem.
textByHash.811b2ec4287b947bda799962e5ab5aea6dcd8114: Right, all done. Ah, and it seems you haven't suffered any permanent damage. That's fortunate.
textByHash.cb80464c05706aa59e8543d2c29d7ff1fef41f37: You have a strong spirit. I assumed you'd be alright. And, after all, it's the spirit tree that really matters. You'll only live for a handful of decades. The tree should live for centuries.
textByHash.8de274ea1665632a7970b99209f1ca34308e2cf0: The welfare of the tree is our concern right now. Something has poisoned the soil, blocking the tree's connection with the Anima Mundi. This is worrying in itself.
textByHash.fb1971cb923e3a1632fa07f0b18a12875d0b5b68: Well, no ordinary poison could do this. Only by separating the Anima Mundi from its source...
textByHash.953905423e7a34407fb8118b9f192c8169318aed: I forget I'm dealing with a human. I'll spell it out nice and clearly for you. The Anima Mundi is created by all natural things. It is their soul, and a sort of natural energy.
textByHash.39a11ceabc5d50b4c5512c8a7b90bd80da895b25: It is powerful and dangerous, and only by the balance of Guthix does it remain in check. Creatures live, creatures die. The balance is maintained.
textByHash.ddaf13ca3d2eb0b9f8a50455863f620c2921bfc5: Powerful magics could be used to separate the Anima Mundi from its source. This would produce a great deal of power, power that could be used in an unbridled way.
textByHash.4448ccfd09f872af202a554c022cded12e83661a: However, doing so would unbalance the whole pattern of life. The ichor in the soil appears to be the unnatural waste product of such tinkerings.
textByHash.52398ff9403c3838efe4f339d22dd31f20dd9044: Most likely, but to make enough of this ichor to create a swamp would take an enormous amount of work and would create a dizzying amount of energy.
textByHash.37713e31994503630c5d2c54b22157576c552c98: It would be an abomination, to plunder the earth in such a massive way. Who would need to do this?
textByHash.6c650c275f1a4e2d82017458ee84d5734250331d: Hmph, maybe. Anyway, our immediate problem is to help our spirit brethren.
textByHash.1116ff0e595c95456f9c802042a8db35b025e073: If you had been paying attention, you'd remember that I said this is no normal poison. Some sort of spiritual cleansing will be needed in order to return the spirit tree's connection to the Anima Mundi.
textByHash.810514068f27b0d22781e2c4fdd192dd2e2fddc9: I do not have the power to do this kind of thing. This will require the magic of Seren and her elves. A pure note, created by elven crystal, would have a purifying effect on anything nearby.
textByHash.cee725d2fd3c73cb50a6b73768c08213e8a31fbd: Luckily, you seem to already be carrying the answer.
textByHash.6839a5930cab686743a8d8fa3a5d75b68e495b48: Use your crystal chime to try and cleanse the tree. Let us hope this works.
textByHash.ea0d2e7b3272099d121c58b1bc15c5b9ed826eec: Luckily, it seems you already encountered the answer below the Tree Gnome Village.
textByHash.3f49a64a299a895995a78a47a10acce94c4154e4: Bring a crystal chime here and use it to try and cleanse the tree.
textByHash.739695f6618d5534a3bc043b0191416ad7409751: I sense that you haven't yet managed to cleanse the tree? What is holding you up?
textByHash.5ae61707eaeacaf2b082d261fe4e920369103edf: You'll need to go and fetch a crystal chime.
textByHash.c079085680ca4be4e5884f7d4ca4f73486a79ae5: What are you doing here? That spirit tree needs our help. Get back there!
textByHash.d1e7868cd3670444d003f91b410d1fc7f770cb2f: Hmm, not a bad job I guess. Now be on your way, I have meditating to do.
textByHash.974b5f5614a7947a9b8c1043346892c2ffa8ba32: What are you doing, stupid human?
textByHash.4e640b7ca202087b2648aa3e15f3162a38fd8aa8: You're not dead yet, fool! Snap out of it!
textByHash.b6ea0d3b39f5fffefc31bed6e71a85bd10c04a88: Pull yourself together! We haven't got much time.
textByHash.26d2522f455ca9db614be4aad959be18ad65e4df: Argento's last gift to me. I was saving it for a special occasion. I suppose saving your hide counts.
textByHash.1d101053683556592f2d8bece1de68ce4c3ec201: It seems I have made my choice. So be it.
textByHash.95a8e0e664f98be2e885897f5c936cf7061475cb: Nothing you need to worry about now. We need to get out of here.
textByHash.1e8d9532e14a3d18bc85af3150ec5645af8ee8e3: Consider it a thank you for helping that spirit tree. You have shown a great empathy towards the Anima Mundi. Despite being a human, perhaps you could one day be a true spirit tree mage.
textByHash.fa291a25e3ba18ae969bedfbf2ae4e519708c683: Bah! I've always been able to speak your uncivilised tongue.
textByHash.b1ffbd6e5f82e5d5e84cce474f93f813a4a11c54: Normally I wouldn't lower myself to these rudimentary words, but given your recent near-death experience, I felt you would not have the energy for a mind meld.
textByHash.70128ac26d19914d89b26679fd9cf1d0086522e9: Perhaps, but it seems I would be wise to ensure your safety. The future is not a place for old wizards like me.
textByHash.99f83cb94eb9fc89eb572f1ca6e222dc08b0adee: My autumn is ending, and only winter remains. Soon, it will fall to individuals like you to protect the Anima Mundi.
textByHash.04adab378f6e8f73bd5fb78fea218a4a8ed887d0: The Anima Mundi granted me a glimpse into the future. I had one final choice to make, and I made it by saving you. We now walk a path that we cannot stray from, so listen closely...
textByHash.9038d2f4712652aa56d83a66964015e098a8b6a2: Skilled brothers, numbering eight, will assemble to fight the Mahjarrat deceiver, and they will be heading northwards to confront him.
textByHash.2bf680dbc6c204b6915885697e45510d9df95e61: The time after this is... clouded to me, but I see you clearly, dressed in the evil garb of the darkest night.
textByHash.09aed67210bc08594577ecb574f1edaa59b5bf26: However, while you may dress in the colours of evil, you will not follow that path. You will remain true to Guthix.
textByHash.83ee7d2ecc499bb737f2890c1fac346e50e60d4b: Of the eight, only two shall return. One represents the path of the slayer and one the path of the warrior.
textByHash.5aeb88a62635b3a3c27606b45d0b656d0dc8c483: Things might have been different, but I have made my choice, and it is up to you to prove that I made the right one.
textByHash.d8f02f6218f0974702af79df17bcfb7a58719286: Dreams often reveal truths masked by the light of day, but I know the truth of what I have seen in my bones. I have seen my end.
textByHash.e344ca56bd61f0f619ae36729563c79dfda5daa3: All I know is what the trees tell me. It will soon be time for me to pass the guardianship of the spirit trees to someone else. Maybe that person is you.
textByHash.69b7e770fb8768a65ce1de5368cc3a6dc1e53815: I have faith that you will learn what you need. Anyway, enough moping about the future. You should be on your way.
textByHash.863a6cb90840ddc8efbfaa60bd03c9037f5df682: Considering you nearly died trying to find him, I suggest you leave that problem for another day. Preferably after a better way has been found into Arposandra.
textByHash.44dbe48d27465ebd4f9c4976667e48640bee5596: Now, I'm sure you have more important matters to attend to than chatting to a cranky old gnome. Take these and be on your way.
textByHash.90df35f8476a3b547cc68f82b93a8e83e581be27: You're back. After these I'm guessing? Take them and stop disturbing me.
textByHash.41e493170d22609d5acd2dcb6f3ff2e31777fa2a: You're back. After this I'm guessing? Take it and stop disturbing me.
textByHash.d1910eb329b48af913eda56c3353762aab0194e8: You're back. Come to disturb me, have you?
textByHash.554f1778b96ed7e55a684f9427423bbafbc1fbfe: Do you actually want something from me, or are you simply making my life a misery again?
textByHash.ad712815273c1c718be8f56d92f09df382ea337c: Well I'm fine, thank you. Now leave me in peace.
textByHash.ed5167ad4db9b7f3ada6502965edc13ffd8f924f: Ah, I see you're already here, human. Good.
textByHash.fee34f64c80fbe837d871d6bf573881d1b5c9c14: Were you not paying attention when we last spoke? We find ourselves on the edge of a very dark future, one where the Mahjarrat deceiver is successful.
textByHash.0964c1ec4a4aad5c87380d1e938c750d10a2eaa5: He is a threat to all life. If he is not stopped, he will warp the Anima Mundi beyond recognition. As a guardian of the Anima Mundi, I must be here to ensure that doesn't happen.
textByHash.204987c24a74e75bc64d5718cf54a18f2f5ff459: The Anima Mundi gave me a choice, and I have made it. If this is to be my last winter, then I go knowing the world will live on to see the next.
textByHash.9a497e06d2fb2b89c3a4326667447cadc3c70aeb: Blah blah blah Guthix blah blah!
textByHash.db61812a2d20cf05712f08435d25f9e22152b0c2: Attempting to perform a mind meld on this many people would be a needless waste of energy, so I find myself forced to use your rudimentary language.
textByHash.af90b78405d6af00179bc579614648e457913f47: Hmph! How much longer are we going to be waiting anyway?
textByHash.0ee6699b6562303f284bc23dd3be71a34655747a: Bah! No one here will have anything of interest to say. I'm better off conversing with myself.
textByHash.5ad978f6769618f3dd7889c1c835bb2493095286: The human will be toast if we don't move now. Ready for teleport!
textByHash.23abe12edb8d7aa4450db8f4610cf9f1d245c611: This decision was already made and was for the best. Goodbye, my friends.
textByHash.070a9cc4344430d446a99d7e7688441b723b63c5: Well, I never expected to be impressed by a human, but you have done a good deed for the gnomes - even if most gnomes won't recognise it. You humans are not as stupid as I first thought...
textByHash.e0aaa22eed931f7c910b85be9c80ad3e8784c576: So you want to know what your crystal seed does?
textByHash.5dc1be161b22c2039e5cd4ea686a5125de8a48e0: The seed is a piece of elven magic, all elven magic comes from the manipulation of seeds of crystal. The elves give it divine properties. Elves sing to their crystals and they form into the desired shape.
textByHash.38161c36130603e4bcb41d805262e7e8be4672b1: Either talk to an elf or find some other way to sing the crystal. If this came from Oaknock, he must have found a way...
textByHash.8ea99687fb8fceb7b0fa4e7163d514167702ed09: So you want to know what your crystal saw does?
textByHash.d3b9106c14837e107eea5132d4519e2366b4a642: Well, I do remember seeing Oaknock using the saw. He said it allowed him to build devices more complicated that even he was capable of constructing. The device he built must have been very complicated indeed.
textByHash.038e8450018667842ad87ca182ca85da89b907a1: You must have had to travel a long way to get this to me. Here's a little something extra as a tip.
